<current_datetime>2026-05-06T01:21:27.902Z</current_datetime>

I have this data-driven test using object arrays. Is there a better way?

```csharp
[TestMethod]
[DynamicData(nameof(TestData))]
public void Validate_ReturnsExpected(string input, bool expected)
{
    var result = Validator.IsValid(input);
    Assert.AreEqual(expected, result);
}

public static IEnumerable<object[]> TestData =>
[
    ["valid@email.com", true],
    ["invalid", false],
    ["", false],
];
```

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a better way to write data-driven tests in MSTest instead of using `object[]` with `DynamicData`. The main improvement is using **strongly-typed tuples** or the newer `[DataRow]` attribute, depending on the scenario.

Let me give a concise answer about the options.
